title: SketchDimensions Object
description: A collection of the dimensions in a sketch. This object also supports the methods to add new sketch dimensions.

methods.name: addAngularDimension
methods.description: Creates a new angular dimension constraint between the two input lines. The position of the text controls which of the four quadrants will be dimensioned.

methods.name: addConcentricCircleDimension
methods.description: Creates a new dimension constraint between to concentric circles or arcs.

methods.name: addDiameterDimension
methods.description: Creates a new diameter dimension constraint on the arc or circle.

methods.name: addDistanceBetweenLineAndPlanarSurfaceDimension
methods.description: Creates a new linear dimension controlling the distance between a sketch line and the specified planar face or construction plane. The sketch line must lie on a plane that is parallel to the planar surface. The text position is automatically chosen and is positioned so it is midway between the line and surface and the extension lines are a minimum length. You can modify the position by using functionality on the returned SketchDistanceBetweenLineAndPlanarSurfaceDimension object.

methods.name: addDistanceBetweenPointAndSurfaceDimension
methods.description: Creates a new linear dimension controlling the distance between a sketch point and the specified surface or point. The text position is automatically chosen and is positioned so it is midway between the point and surface and the extension lines are a minimum length. You can modify the position by using functionality on the returned SketchDistanceBetweenPointAndSurfaceDimension object.

methods.name: addDistanceDimension
methods.description: Creates a new linear dimension constraint between the two input entities.

methods.name: addEllipseMajorRadiusDimension
methods.description: Creates a new dimension constraint on the major radius of an ellipse.

methods.name: addEllipseMinorRadiusDimension
methods.description: Creates a new dimension constraint on the minor radius of an ellipse.

methods.name: addLinearDiameterDimension
methods.description: Creates a new linear dimension showing the diameter where the first line acts as the center line and the second entity defines the size. The first input entity must be a sketch line. The second entity can be a point or a line that is parallel to the first. The dimension controls the distance as measured perpendicular to the first input line.

methods.name: addOffsetDimension
methods.description: Creates a new linear dimension constraint between the two input entities. The first input entity must be a sketch line. The second entity can be a point or a line that is parallel to the first. The dimension controls the distance as measured perpendicular to the first input line.

methods.name: addRadialDimension
methods.description: Creates a new radial dimension constraint on the arc or circle.

methods.name: addTangentDistanceDimension
methods.description: Creates a new linear dimension from between a line and circle or arc and a second circle or arc where the dimension is to the tangent on the edge of the circle or arc.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified sketch dimension using an index into the collection.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of sketch dimensions in the sketch.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: API Sample that demonstrates creating sketch lines in various ways.
related_samples.description: Demonstrates several ways to create sketch lines, including as the result of creating a rectangle.

addAngularDimension.title: SketchDimensions.addAngularDimension Method
addAngularDimension.description: Creates a new angular dimension constraint between the two input lines. The position of the text controls which of the four quadrants will be dimensioned.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addAngularDimension(lineOne, lineTwo, textPoint)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addAngularDimension(lineOne, lineTwo, textPoint, isDriving)

addAngularDimension.return_description: Returns the newly created dimension or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: lineOne
parameters.description: The first SketchLine to dimension to.

parameters.name: lineTwo
parameters.description: The second SketchLine to dimension to.

parameters.name: textPoint
parameters.description: A Point3D object that defines the position of the dimension text. The position of the text also defines which quadrant will be dimensioned.

parameters.name: isDriving
parameters.description: Optional argument that specifies if a driving (the dimension controls the geometry) or a driven (the geometry controls the dimension) dimension is created. If not provided a driving dimension will be created.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

addConcentricCircleDimension.title: SketchDimensions.addConcentricCircleDimension Method
addConcentricCircleDimension.description: Creates a new dimension constraint between to concentric circles or arcs.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addConcentricCircleDimension(circleOne, circleTwo, textPoint)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addConcentricCircleDimension(circleOne, circleTwo, textPoint, isDriving)

addConcentricCircleDimension.return_description: Returns the newly created dimension or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: circleOne
parameters.description: The first SketchCircle or SketchArc to dimension.

parameters.name: circleTwo
parameters.description: The second SketchCircle or SketchArc to dimension.

parameters.name: textPoint
parameters.description: A Point3D object that defines the position of the dimension text.

parameters.name: isDriving
parameters.description: Optional argument that specifies if a driving (the dimension controls the geometry) or a driven (the geometry controls the dimension) dimension is created. If not provided a driving dimension will be created.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

addDiameterDimension.title: SketchDimensions.addDiameterDimension Method
addDiameterDimension.description: Creates a new diameter dimension constraint on the arc or circle.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addDiameterDimension(entity, textPoint)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addDiameterDimension(entity, textPoint, isDriving)

addDiameterDimension.return_description: Returns the newly created dimension or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The SketchCircle or SketchArc to dimension.

parameters.name: textPoint
parameters.description: A Point3D object that defines the position of the dimension text.

parameters.name: isDriving
parameters.description: Optional argument that specifies if a driving (the dimension controls the geometry) or a driven (the geometry controls the dimension) dimension is created. If not provided a driving dimension will be created.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

addDistanceBetweenLineAndPlanarSurfaceDi.title: SketchDimensions.addDistanceBetweenLineAndPlanarSurfaceDimension Method
addDistanceBetweenLineAndPlanarSurfaceDi.description: Creates a new linear dimension controlling the distance between a sketch line and the specified planar face or construction plane. The sketch line must lie on a plane that is parallel to the planar surface. The text position is automatically chosen and is positioned so it is midway between the line and surface and the extension lines are a minimum length. You can modify the position by using functionality on the returned SketchDistanceBetweenLineAndPlanarSurfaceDimension object.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addDistanceBetweenLineAndPlanarSurfaceDimension(line, planarSurface)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addDistanceBetweenLineAndPlanarSurfaceDimension(line, planarSurface, isDriving)

addDistanceBetweenLineAndPlanarSurfaceDi.return_description: Returns the newly created dimension or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: line
parameters.description: The SketchLine being constrained by the dimension.

parameters.name: planarSurface
parameters.description: The planar BRepFace or ConstructionPlane that the dimension will anchor to.

parameters.name: isDriving
parameters.description: Optional argument that specifies if a driving (the dimension controls the geometry) or a driven (the geometry controls the dimension) dimension is created. If not provided a driving dimension will be created.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

addDistanceBetweenPointAndSurfaceDimensi.title: SketchDimensions.addDistanceBetweenPointAndSurfaceDimension Method
addDistanceBetweenPointAndSurfaceDimensi.description: Creates a new linear dimension controlling the distance between a sketch point and the specified surface or point. The text position is automatically chosen and is positioned so it is midway between the point and surface and the extension lines are a minimum length. You can modify the position by using functionality on the returned SketchDistanceBetweenPointAndSurfaceDimension object.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addDistanceBetweenPointAndSurfaceDimension(point, surface)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addDistanceBetweenPointAndSurfaceDimension(point, surface, isDriving)

addDistanceBetweenPointAndSurfaceDimensi.return_description: Returns the newly created dimension or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The SketchPoint being constrained by the dimension.

parameters.name: surface
parameters.description: The BRepFace or ConstructionPlane to which the dimension will anchor. Planar, cylindrical, spherical and conical faces are supported. If a cylindrical, spherical or conical face is used, the dimension is from the point to the nearest point on the surface.

parameters.name: isDriving
parameters.description: Optional argument that specifies if a driving (the dimension controls the geometry) or a driven (the geometry controls the dimension) dimension is created. If not provided a driving dimension will be created.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

addDistanceDimension.title: SketchDimensions.addDistanceDimension Method
addDistanceDimension.description: Creates a new linear dimension constraint between the two input entities.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addDistanceDimension(pointOne, pointTwo, orientation, textPoint)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addDistanceDimension(pointOne, pointTwo, orientation, textPoint, isDriving)

addDistanceDimension.return_description: Returns the newly created dimension or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: pointOne
parameters.description: The first SketchPoint to dimension to.

parameters.name: pointTwo
parameters.description: The second SketchPoint to dimension to..

parameters.name: orientation
parameters.description: The orientation of the dimension.

parameters.name: textPoint
parameters.description: A Point3D object that defines the position of the dimension text.

parameters.name: isDriving
parameters.description: Optional argument that specifies if a driving (the dimension controls the geometry) or a driven (the geometry controls the dimension) dimension is created. If not provided a driving dimension will be created.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

addLinearDiameterDimension.title: SketchDimensions.addLinearDiameterDimension Method
addLinearDiameterDimension.description: Creates a new linear dimension showing the diameter where the first line acts as the center line and the second entity defines the size. The first input entity must be a sketch line. The second entity can be a point or a line that is parallel to the first. The dimension controls the distance as measured perpendicular to the first input line.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addLinearDiameterDimension(centerLine, entityTwo, textPoint)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addLinearDiameterDimension(centerLine, entityTwo, textPoint, isDriving)

addLinearDiameterDimension.return_description: Returns the newly created dimension or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: centerLine
parameters.description: The SketchLine to dimension to which acts as the center line.

parameters.name: entityTwo
parameters.description: The parallel SketchLine or SketchPoint to dimension to. If a SketchLine is used it must be parallel to the first line.

parameters.name: textPoint
parameters.description: A Point3D object that defines the position of the dimension text.

parameters.name: isDriving
parameters.description: Optional argument that specifies if a driving (the dimension controls the geometry) or a driven (the geometry controls the dimension) dimension is created. If not provided a driving dimension will be created.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

addOffsetDimension.title: SketchDimensions.addOffsetDimension Method
addOffsetDimension.description: Creates a new linear dimension constraint between the two input entities. The first input entity must be a sketch line. The second entity can be a point or a line that is parallel to the first. The dimension controls the distance as measured perpendicular to the first input line.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addOffsetDimension(line, entityTwo, textPoint)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addOffsetDimension(line, entityTwo, textPoint, isDriving)

addOffsetDimension.return_description: Returns the newly created dimension or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: line
parameters.description: The SketchLine to dimension to.

parameters.name: entityTwo
parameters.description: The parallel SketchLine or SketchPoint to dimension to. If a SketchLine is used it must be parallel to the first line.

parameters.name: textPoint
parameters.description: A Point3D object that defines the position of the dimension text.

parameters.name: isDriving
parameters.description: Optional argument that specifies if a driving (the dimension controls the geometry) or a driven (the geometry controls the dimension) dimension is created. If not provided a driving dimension will be created.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

addRadialDimension.description: Creates a new radial dimension constraint on the arc or circle.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addRadialDimension(entity, textPoint)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addRadialDimension(entity, textPoint, isDriving)

addRadialDimension.return_description: Returns the newly created dimension or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The SketchCircle or SketchArc to dimension.

parameters.name: textPoint
parameters.description: A Point3D object that defines the position of the dimension text.

parameters.name: isDriving
parameters.description: Optional argument that specifies if a driving (the dimension controls the geometry) or a driven (the geometry controls the dimension) dimension is created. If not provided a driving dimension will be created.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

addTangentDistanceDimension.title: SketchDimensions.addTangentDistanceDimension Method
addTangentDistanceDimension.description: Creates a new linear dimension from between a line and circle or arc and a second circle or arc where the dimension is to the tangent on the edge of the circle or arc.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addTangentDistanceDimension(entityOne, isCloseToEnityTwo, entityTwo, isCloseToEnityOne, textPoint)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchDimensions_var.addTangentDistanceDimension(entityOne, isCloseToEnityTwo, entityTwo, isCloseToEnityOne, textPoint, isDriving)

addTangentDistanceDimension.return_description: Returns the newly created dimension or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: entityOne
parameters.description: The first entity to dimension to. This can be a SketchPoint, SketchLine, SketchCircle or SketchArc. If a circle or arc is provided then the tangentSideOne argument must be specified.

parameters.name: isCloseToEnityTwo
parameters.description: If entityOne is a circle or arc this specifies which side of the circle or arc the dimension will be tangent to. If true, it will be on the side that is closer to entityTwo. If a SketchLine or SketchPoint was input for the entityOne argument this argument is ignored and any value can be used.

parameters.name: entityTwo
parameters.description: A SketchCircle or SketchArc entity that you will dimension to.

parameters.name: isCloseToEnityOne
parameters.description: Specifies which side of the circle or arc input as the entityTwo argument the dimension will be tangent to. If true, it will be on the side that is closer to entityOne.

parameters.name: textPoint
parameters.description: A Point3D object that defines the position of the dimension text.

parameters.name: isDriving
parameters.description: Optional argument that specifies if a driving (the dimension controls the geometry) or a driven (the geometry controls the dimension) dimension is created. If not provided a driving dimension will be created.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

classType.title: SketchDimensions.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: SketchDimensions.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified sketch dimension using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

count.title: SketchDimensions.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of sketch dimensions in the sketch.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchDimensions_var.count

isValid.title: SketchDimensions.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchDimensions_var.isValid

objectType.title: SketchDimensions.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchDimensions_var.objectType
